name: com.coachfit.calendar.adapter.out.persistence.CalendarEventPersistenceAdapter
comment:
JPA + JdbcClient adapter implementing {@link CalendarEventPersistencePort}.

Simple reads use Spring Data JPA via {@link CalendarEventJpaRepository}. Bulk / complex writes use {@link JdbcClient} for fine-grained SQL control.

name: com.coachfit.calendar.application.port.out.CalendarEventPersistencePort
comment:
Output port: calendar event persistence.

All write operations that take a {@code userId} enforce ownership so a user can never mutate another user's events.

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(java.util.UUID, java.util.UUID, java.time.LocalDate, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.UUID)
methods.comment: Updates mutable fields of an existing (non-deleted) event.

methods.name: updateStatus
methods.comment: Transitions the event's status if the event exists and belongs to the user.

methods.name: linkActivity
methods.comment: Links a completed activity to an event and sets compliance score. Also transitions status to 'completed' (or 'partial' if score < 50).

methods.name: unlinkActivity
methods.comment: Unlinks an activity from an event, resetting status to 'planned'.

methods.name: reorder
methods.comment: Reassigns {@code order_index} values in batch. Each entry: (eventId, newOrderIndex). Only updates rows owned by userId.

methods.name: findPlannedWorkoutsByDate
methods.comment: Finds planned uncompleted workout events for a user on a specific date.

methods.name: findUserTimezone
methods.comment: Finds the user's timezone from their settings, defaulting to Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh or UTC.

methods.name: autoSkipPastPlanned
methods.comment: Auto-skip: find all planned events with date < today and mark them skipped. Called by the nightly scheduler.

methods.name: findActivityDetails
methods.comment: Finds duration and sport details of an activity.

name: com.coachfit.calendar.application.service.CalendarAutoSkipScheduler
comment:
Nightly scheduled job that auto-skips past planned calendar events.

Per docs/05-api-design.md:

Auto-skip rule: Calendar events with status='planned' and date < today are auto-marked 'skipped' by a scheduled job running at 23:59 UTC daily. Coach can override.

The scheduler runs at 23:59 UTC every day via a cron expression. It delegates directly to {@link CalendarEventPersistencePort#autoSkipPastPlanned()} which executes a single bulk UPDATE — no per-event processing overhead.

Spring Scheduling must be enabled via {@code @EnableScheduling} on the application class or a {@code @Configuration} class.

methods.name: autoSkipPastEvents
methods.comment:
Runs at 23:59 UTC every day.

Marks all {@code planned} events whose {@code date} is before today as {@code skipped}. Returns the number of rows updated (logged at INFO level).

name: com.coachfit.calendar.application.service.CalendarEventService
comment:
Application service implementing all calendar event use cases.

State machine (from docs/05-api-design.md): 

 planned   → completed : activity linked (compliance ≥ 50%)      [linkActivity] planned   → partial   : activity linked (compliance < 50%)       [linkActivity] planned   → skipped   : user/coach explicit skip OR auto-skip    [skip] completed → planned   : linked activity deleted                  [internal] partial   → completed : manual override                          [complete] partial   → planned   : linked activity deleted                  [internal] skipped   → planned   : user un-skips                            [skip(unskip=true)]

Ownership is enforced at every mutating query; userId is always included in WHERE clauses so a user can never modify another user's events.

methods.name: complete
methods.comment: Transitions {@code partial} → {@code completed}. Per the state machine: only partial events can be manually completed.

methods.name: findOwnedEvent
methods.comment: Loads a calendar event and verifies it belongs to the given user. Throws 404 if not found or belongs to another user.

methods.name: toView
methods.comment: Maps the persistence summary to the API read model. Workout and activity nested objects are resolved by the controller layer (currently passed as null — enrichment deferred to a future cross-module join).
